messages.subject: [R-jobs] summer internship at Novartis
messages.body_plain:
Dear R coders,

The Modeling & Simulation department at Novartis is seeking a summer
intern to complete development on an R package.

Description:
* Complete development, enhancements and release/publication of the
simR package and related projects:
(http://user2010.org/abstracts/Kahn+Danenberg+Das+Ayers.pdf)

* Internship requires a commitment of approximately 12 weeks, full time.

Requirements:
* Ideal intern should be an excellent R coder versed in OOP, able to
work on a mixture of Windows and Linux/clusters

For more info, please see the description and apply at the link below.
Best regards,
- Joseph

https://sjobs.brassring.com/2057/asp/tg/cim_jobdetail.asp?jobId=1408375&PartnerId=13617&SiteId=5260&type=mail&JobReqLang=1&recordstart=1&JobSiteId=5268&JobSiteInfo=1408375_5268&gqid=0

messages.subject: [R-jobs] JOB: Statistical and Mathematical Modelling in the Energy and Environment Sectors
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] JOB: Statistical and Mathematical Modelling in the Energy and Environment Sectors
messages.body_plain:
JOB: Statistical and Mathematical Modelling in the Energy and Environment
Sectors

Quintessa (www.quintessa.org) is an employee-owned company that undertakes
high-quality science-based consultancy, research and software development
for industry and governments around the world in the energy and environment
sectors. Statistical and mathematical modelling of complex systems (ranging
from geological storage facilities for carbon dioxide to the graphite cores
of Advanced Gas-cooled Reactors) represents an important part of the
company's activities. We would like to hear from individuals interested in
joining Quintessa's office in Henley-on-Thames to contribute to our work in
these areas.  We are looking for someone with a first class academic record,
good presentation skills, and a proven ability to solve problems using
statistical and mathematical modelling techniques. Candidates, who must
already be living in the UK, should send a CV with a covering letter by
e-mail to DavidHodgkinson at quintessa.org.

-- 
Dr David P Hodgkinson, Managing Director, Quintessa Ltd
The Hub, 14 Station Road, Henley-on-Thames, Oxfordshire RG9 1AY, UK
Tel: 01491 636246  DD: 01491 630041 Fax: 01491 636247

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Data Scientist opportunity
messages.body_plain:
>
> Hi All,
>
> We have a great opportunity (DC Metro area) to be part of a new product
> leveraging existing predictive analytics technologies to be applied to
> potentially different vertical markets/fields.  We're seeking several
> experienced consultants to help including this key role.  US Citizenship
> required due to access to sensitive data.  If interested please get in touch
> for more details:
>
> *Data Scientist* - Python and R experience required; Hadoop ecosystem,
> MongoDB, Neo4j, etc... Visualization, Inference or Recommendation tool
> experience in any of a number of different vertical markets also desired
> (e.g., online retail, content, etc.);  M.S. in Mathematics, Statistics,
> Political Science, Physics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering,
> Computer Engineering or equivalent work experience
>
> Please provide links to stackoverflow.com submissions, and GitHub
> projects...
>
> Thanks,
>  Rocky Mehta

Hi,

I am not sure if a rule for this mailing list already exist or not, but it
would be nice to have an idea in which city the position refers by just
reading the email. And I am sorry but "DC metro" doesn't mean anything for
me.
(I am also very sorry but this list is not only for USA and USA is not the
center if the universe so we don't necessarily have to know the states
abreviations)

Thanks

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Opening for a Research Statistician
messages.body_plain:
Research Statistician

Job Summary / General Description
Savvysherpa, LLC collaborates with its partners to do behavioral
research, quantitative analysis, and product and process
innovation. We are looking for a statistician to join our team of
research scientists.

Primary functions and responsibilities
- Designing experiments and analyzing experimental and historical
  data.

Desired Skills/Attributes

Candidates for this position should:
- Experience in screening and factorial design
- Regression analysis
- Categorical data analysis 
- Non-parametric analysis 
- Discrete distributions is desired 
- Thoroughly understand all core statistical concepts 
- Excel at explaining these concepts to others

Requirements
- M.S. or Ph.D. in statistics with 5+ years experience 
- Experience working as a scientific or statistical consultant,
  either as an internal or external resource preferred.
- Experience with a statistical package is required, preferably R or
  SAS, as well as a basic understanding of data architecture and
  SQL.

Please email us at careers at savvysherpa.com to apply.
http://www.savvysherpa.com/pages/Careers.html

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Noble America Announcement for Summer Internship 2011
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Noble America Announcement for Summer Internship 2011
messages.body_plain:
#####################################################

Noble America Announcement for Summer Internship 2011 

######################################################

Organization Name: Noble America, http://www.thisisnoble.com/   

Location: Stamford, Connecticut, USA 

Period: 10-12 Weeks for Summer 2011 starting May/June 2011

Brief Description of work: 


The summer intern will work on building tools for the Trading/Risk groups, and would require:

1. Strong knowledge of R and C# programming 
2. Strong knowledge of time series (more generally mathematical and statistical knowledge is welcome but not strictly necessary) 
3. Financial engineering knowledge, specifically in the area of derivative pricing, is a great plus.  General Finance knowledge is _not_ required.

Typical tasks include:

1. Helping with the testing and building of new systems, tools, models, etc for commodity trading desks 
2. Documentation of existing tools, models, etc

The typical intern may expect to work closely with Risk and Trading and gain some valuable experience in the makings of tools and analysis pertaining to commodities trading (especially Natural Gas and Power)

======================================================

Application: Please send CV by email

For information please address :

Wang Yu (WangYu at thisisnoble.com) and Yannis Tzamouranis (yct at thisisnoble.com)
